title: If Rei transcends time after becoming a God wouldnt she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality
display_title: If Rei transcends time after becoming a God wouldnt she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by KRandMJ » Fri Feb 19, 2016 1:44 am If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality? (Why would she grant Shinji's wish, knowing that he would reject it?) It's widely accepted that in (The End of Evangelion) 1) Rei and Adam merged becoming a God-like entity transcending time, sending Avatars of itself throughout history. (Seen before and after the "God" existed) SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 19, 2016 3:13 am That's a tricky question to answer. It's certainly not explained concretely in the film or show just what is going on there, so to a certain extent you just have to come up with an answer beyond the obvious that works for you, or as we call it around here 'fanwank'. There are several possible options, and perhaps more besides these, so take your pick: She's like Dr. Manhattan in Watchmen, and even though she knows what will happen she's bound to follow the course destiny has drawn. She knows that ultimately he needed this to happen for some reason we aren't shown in the future. There are many possible branches in time, and in different words he wanted different things, and thus both accepted or denied all such options. She didn't transcend time at all, just space. She could always be in multiple places at one time. Rei ascended in stages, and didn't leave the fixed flow of time behind until she died and passed the job to Yui Personally I go with the last one, but the choice is yours. You'll probably find agreement from somebody no matter which you choose. Eva is nifty like that.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 19, 2016 3:15 am Gods are beyond our comprehension, so we wouldn't understand. Shinji needs to go through the process for his own good.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 19, 2016 3:16 am ...lame cop-out, man.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 19, 2016 3:17 am Go down to your local church and see what they say... (BTDT, gave up)

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Feb 19, 2016 3:28 am KRandMJ wrote: It's widely accepted that in (The End of Evangelion)

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 19, 2016 4:58 am Rei wanted to something for Shinji's good, so even if she knew, he'd reject Instrumentality, she'd also know, that he would only come to that conclusion, when confronted with Instrumentality. So if she decided to just not do Instrumentality*, Shinji would never have the that realization. Also, if she wouldn't go through with Instrumentality, Gendou would just trigger another one, some years down the line. *wich I doubt was in her hands after a certain point anyways. Remember, Angel + Adam results in Impact without fail, so I'm sure as soon as Rei merged with Lilith, the ceremony just went on and on, because Shinji was the one controlling the ritual.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 19, 2016 5:24 am KRandMJ wrote: God-Rei is aware of the past and future, thus she knows that Shinji would reject Instrumentality. Why would she allow for instrumentality in the first place?

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 19, 2016 2:17 pm Shinji only rejects it after he experiences it, thus Rei must allow it to happen initially. Perhaps she argues and questions his decision because she knows it's not his true desire, instead of deciding, "Welp, he told everyone to die. Just gonna do it. Okay done individuality is over."

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Feb 21, 2016 11:11 am Well this one would certainly go with the rest of the movie. Shinji had to see instrumentality in order to understand what he had done and what to expect. He doesn't reject Instrumentality until after he had seen all of it. So maybe she knew she had to do this for him so he would change for the better? NemZ wrote: [*]She knows that ultimately he needed this to happen for some reason we aren't shown in the future.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by KRandMJ » Sun Feb 21, 2016 2:33 pm Bagheera wrote: The fact that it is widely accepted does not make it correct. Apart from the problem you cite there is no discernible reason why the merger of two Seeds of Life should result in a being that is able to transcend time and space. In fact, given the demonstrated abilities of a baseline SoL there is no particular reason to think such a merger would extend their capabilities much at all. As a counterargument I would offer that both Lilith and Adam could always do this, i.e. that they were always connected to "their" souls, but that Lilith was unaware of this capability while housed within Rei (meaning soul collection/redistribution was something she did by reflex and not via conscious effort). This is not a popular alternative to the mainstream common wisdom, but it does avoid most of said theory's major pitfalls.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Feb 21, 2016 2:40 pm SoL = Seed of Life. Adam and Lilith are two of the seeds of life which the FAR (First Ancestral Race) sent out into the universe. To limit their capabilities, each seed had only one of the Fruit of Life and Fruit of Knowledge. These were never meant to meet up; but they did when Lilith landed on the same world as Adam. The Lance of Longinus (LoL) was supposed to be a safety device to protect against the consequences if this should happen by keeping at least one of the seeds dormant.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Feb 21, 2016 3:29 pm KRandMJ wrote: How would you interpret the various Rei sightings?

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by KRandMJ » Sun Feb 21, 2016 3:42 pm Bagheera wrote: The first is curiosity; Rei knows this person is important but she doesn't know why, and she doesn't even realize she's visiting him. The second would be her saying goodbye, just as Shinji's mother did in the previous scene.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Feb 21, 2016 4:07 pm KRandMJ wrote: You mean Rei went back in time to visit him?

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Feb 21, 2016 4:49 pm Bagheera wrote: The first is curiosity; Rei knows this person is important but she doesn't know why, and she doesn't even realize she's visiting him. The second would be her saying goodbye, just as Shinji's mother did in the previous scene.

[]: Re: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Feb 21, 2016 4:56 pm NemZ wrote: The first one is a stretch, but the second one is flat-out impossible without either some time shenanigans or making her a ghost or something. This last appearance is AFTER she's been 'dead' for some extended period of time.
summary: []: If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality?             Quote Post by KRandMJ » Fri Feb 19, 2016 1:44 am If Rei transcends time after becoming a "God", wouldn't she know that Shinji would reject Instrumentality? (Why would she grant Shinji's wish, knowing that he would reject it?) It's widely accepted that in (The End of Evangelion) 1) Rei and Adam merged
